commonProblems.h2: Nissan Juke Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Nissan Juke's MR16DDT turbo and H5FT 1.2 DIG-T engines dominate replacement demand due to timing chain and bearing failures. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Juke.

problems.group: MR16DDT Timing Chain & Rod Bearing Failure
problems.h4: MR16DDT Timing Chain & Rod Bearing Failure - Why Juke 1.6 Turbo Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Juke 1.6 DIG-T, Nismo, Nismo RS, Tekna 1.6 DIG-T (2010-2019, MR16DDT)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain slack, causing rattling; oil starvation from clogged feed lines accelerates rod bearing wear leading to catastrophic bottom-end failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, guides, tensioner, VVT sprockets
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before rattling; 40k-60k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace rod bearings, inspect crank, renew oil pump
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if block/crank undamaged; not viable post-knock

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt unit with upgraded bearings, chain kit, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value post-catastrophic failure; 12-month warranty included

problems.recommendation: If you hear timing chain rattle on cold start but no knocking, a preventative chain kit from a specialist offers the best value. Once knocking occurs, rod bearing damage is likely - replacement with a rebuilt MR16DDT is more cost-effective than attempting a bottom-end rebuild.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt MR16DDT engine prices for your Nissan Juke

problems.h4: H5FT 1.2 DIG-T Timing Chain Stretch - Facelift Juke Engine Rattle
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Juke 1.2 DIG-T Visia, Acenta, N-Connecta, Tekna (2014-2019, H5FT)
problems.rootCause: Inadequate chain tensioner design allows premature stretch; combined with turbo oil feed restrictions, this causes rattling, misfires, and potential valve timing loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides, VVT components
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended at first sign of rattle; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1.2 DIG-T with upgraded tensioner, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if valve timing lost or engine seized; warranty included

problems.recommendation: The 1.2 DIG-T's timing chain failure is often caught early due to distinctive rattling. If diagnosed before valve interference occurs, a specialist chain kit replacement is cost-effective. If the engine has seized or lost compression, replacement is the only viable route.
problems.cta: Get quotes for H5FT Nissan Juke engine replacement

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Blocked Oil Feed on MR16DDT & H5FT
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup restricts turbo oil feed line, starving bearings of lubrication; leads to shaft play, smoke, and loss of boost.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit new/recon turbo, renew oil feed line, flush system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine undamaged; requires thorough oil system clean

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt engine with new turbo, upgraded oil feed, full system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo failure caused oil starvation damage to bearings

problems.recommendation: If turbo failure is isolated (no engine knock, good compression), replacing just the turbo with an upgraded oil feed line is cost-effective. However, if oil starvation has damaged bearings, a full rebuilt engine with new turbo offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Compare turbo-ready rebuilt Juke engine prices

problems.h4: K9K 1.5 dCi EGR & DPF Issues - Diesel Juke Reliability
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Juke 1.5 dCi Visia, Acenta, Tekna (2010-2019, K9K)
problems.rootCause: EGR cooler clogging restricts exhaust flow; combined with short-journey DPF regeneration failures, this causes power loss, warning lights, and eventual engine derate.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Blank EGR valve, clean/replace DPF, ECU remap for fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective for urban drivers; may affect MOT emissions compliance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler, DPF sensor, renew associated hoses
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if retaining emissions compliance; extends engine life

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt K9K Diesel Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K9K with updated EGR mapping, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine damaged by prolonged derate or oil dilution

problems.recommendation: For Juke diesels used primarily on short journeys, an EGR delete with DPF cleaning offers the most practical solution. If the engine has suffered oil dilution or bearing wear from prolonged derate, a rebuilt K9K is more reliable long-term.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K9K Nissan Juke diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: HR16DE Ignition & Cooling Failures - Base Engine Issues
problems.rootCause: Ignition coil pack degradation causes misfires; thermostat sticking leads to overheating and potential head gasket failure if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four coil packs, renew thermostat and housing
repairOptions.longevity: Highly cost-effective; resolves most misfire/overheat issues

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, machine surface, renew bolts
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block undamaged; requires specialist machining

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt NA engine with upgraded cooling components
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if head warped or block damaged by overheating

problems.recommendation: The HR16DE is generally robust; most issues are resolved with coil and thermostat replacement. Only consider engine replacement if overheating caused significant head warping or block damage - otherwise, repair is the economical choice.
problems.cta: Compare HR16DE Nissan Juke engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan Juke
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with MR16DDT timing chain rattle, H5FT turbo failure, or K9K DPF issues, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Nissan Juke specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan Juke Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan Juke and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

types.title: Nissan Juke Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Professionally cleaned, inspected and lightly rebuilt units with replaced wear items like seals and gaskets.
types.frontDescription: Professionally cleaned, inspected and lightly rebuilt units with replaced wear items like seals and gaskets.
types.frontDisclaimer: Suitable for engines with minor wear; full inspection report provided.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned Juke engines undergo thorough cleaning, compression testing and replacement of consumables. Ideal for MR16DDT or K9K units with good compression but high mileage.
backBullets: Includes new gaskets, seals, oil pump and water pump
Bench-tested prior to dispatch with 12-month warranty
Fast turnaround: typically 3-5 working days from order

types.title: Nissan Juke Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Fully stripped, machined and rebuilt with upgraded components to address known failure points like timing chains.
types.frontDescription: Fully stripped, machined and rebuilt with upgraded components to address known failure points like timing chains.
types.frontDisclaimer: Best value for catastrophic failures; includes upgraded bearings and chain kits.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt Juke engines are the most popular choice for MR16DDT and H5FT failures. All critical components are renewed with upgraded parts to prevent repeat issues.

Dyno-tested and supplied with full build documentation

engineSizes.h2: Nissan Juke Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Nissan Juke is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Nissan Juke 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L K9K diesel powered most first-generation Juke diesel variants from launch through 2019. A Renault-sourced unit favoured for economy, it's common in fleet and high-mileage examples but requires attention to EGR and DPF maintenance.
commonFailurePoints: DPF regeneration failure on short journeys
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Juke 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Nissan Juke 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced with the second-generation Juke in 2020, the 1.0L HR10DDT is a downsized three-cylinder turbo focused on efficiency. Early units may experience timing chain tensioner wear but overall reliability is improving.
items.cta: Compare Nissan Juke 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Juke 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L H5FT DIG-T turbo arrived with the 2014 facelift as a downsized alternative to the 1.6. Compact and efficient but prone to timing chain stretch and turbo oil feed issues requiring proactive maintenance.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain stretch causing cold-start rattle
items.cta: Compare Nissan Juke 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Juke 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L family includes both the naturally aspirated HR16DE (base models) and turbocharged MR16DDT (performance variants). The MR16DDT dominates replacement demand due to timing chain and rod bearing failures.
commonFailurePoints: turbo oil starvation; HR16DE: ignition coil failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Juke 1.6L petrol engines

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Nissan Juke needs? items.title: Nissan Juke Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L K9K diesel powered first-generation Juke variants from 2010-2019, favoured for economy but requiring proactive EGR and DPF maintenance.
items.descriptor: The 1.5L K9K diesel powered first-generation Juke variants from 2010-2019, favoured for economy but requiring proactive EGR and DPF maintenance.
items.familiesLabel: Common Nissan Juke Diesel Engine Families
families: K9K 636 - Early 1.5 dCi (Visia/Acenta), EGR cooler clogging common
K9K 764 - Updated Euro 6 variant (facelift), turbo actuator issues reported
K9K 866 - Late-production spec, improved oil pump design
knownFor: Strong fuel economy (55-65 mpg combined)
EGR and DPF issues on short-journey vehicles
High UK replacement demand on fleet/high-mileage examples
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Juke Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Nissan Juke Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Jukes span naturally aspirated 1.6L base units to turbocharged 1.2L and 1.6L performance engines, with MR16DDT timing chain issues driving most replacement enquiries.
items.descriptor: Petrol Jukes span naturally aspirated 1.6L base units to turbocharged 1.2L and 1.6L performance engines, with MR16DDT timing chain issues driving most replacement enquiries.
items.familiesLabel: Common Nissan Juke Petrol Engine Families
families: MR16DDT - 1.6 turbo (190-215hp), timing chain & rod bearing failures dominant
H5FT - 1.2 DIG-T turbo (115hp), timing chain stretch and turbo oil feed issues
HR16DE - 1.6 NA (117hp), reliable but coil pack and thermostat failures common
HR10DDT - 1.0 DIG-T turbo (117hp), second-gen only, early tensioner wear
knownFor: MR16DDT: High performance but timing chain/rod bearing vulnerability
H5FT: Efficient but requires proactive chain maintenance
HR16DE: Robust base engine with simple, affordable repairs
items.cta: Compare Nissan Juke Petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Juke Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: Second-generation Juke (2020+) offers 12V mild-hybrid assistance on 1.0 DIG-T engines for improved efficiency and stop-start refinement.
items.descriptor: Second-generation Juke (2020+) offers 12V mild-hybrid assistance on 1.0 DIG-T engines for improved efficiency and stop-start refinement.
families: HR10DDT MHEV - 1.0 turbo with 12V belt-driven starter-generator
12V lithium-ion battery powers enhanced stop-start and torque assist
Energy recovered during braking/deceleration stored for acceleration support
No electric-only driving; engine replacement requires hybrid system inspection
importantNotes: MHEV components add complexity; ensure specialist handles replacement
Battery health check recommended during engine replacement
Availability more limited than standard HR10DDT units

engineYears.h2: Nissan Juke Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Juke's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: F15 Launch: MR16DDT Turbo & HR16DE NA Debut
years.description: First-generation Juke launched with turbo and naturally aspirated 1.6 petrol options, establishing the MR16DDT as the performance benchmark.
keyChanges: Juke F15 platform introduced with distinctive crossover styling
MR16DDT 1.6 turbo (190hp) and HR16DE 1.6 NA (117hp) engines available
Front-wheel drive standard; intelligent AWD optional on turbo models
mainEngines: MR16DDT - 1.6L petrol turbo (Acenta DIG-T, Tekna DIG-T, 4WD)
knownFor: MR16DDT praised for performance but early timing chain concerns emerge
HR16DE noted for simplicity and lower running costs
AWD system adds weight but improves traction in poor conditions
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2012 Nissan Juke replacement engines
years.ticker: Nissan Juke 2010 engine replacement • MR16DDT timing chain rattle • Juke 1.6 turbo won't start

years.preview: F15 Update: Nismo Performance Variant Introduced
years.description: Nismo trim launched with uprated MR16DDT producing 197hp, creating a new performance segment for Juke engine replacements.
keyChanges: Nismo variant introduced with sport-tuned MR16DDT (197hp)
Revised suspension, body kit and interior for Nismo trim
K9K 1.5 dCi diesel added to range for fleet/commercial buyers
knownFor: Nismo MR16DDT sought after for performance upgrades
K9K diesel popular with high-mileage/fleet users
Early MR16DDT timing chain failures begin appearing in forums
years.cta: Compare Nismo-spec Nissan Juke engine prices
years.ticker: Nissan Juke Nismo engine • K9K diesel replacement • MR16DDT rod bearing failure

years.description: Facelift introduced the downsized 1.2L H5FT turbo and updated MR16DDT with revised oil pump design to address early failures.
keyChanges: 1.2L H5FT DIG-T turbo (115hp) added as new entry-level petrol option
MR16DDT received updated oil pump and tensioner design
Exterior refresh with new LED lighting and interior tech upgrades
mainEngines: H5FT - 1.2L petrol turbo (115hp, new for facelift)
knownFor: H5FT timing chain stretch becomes new common failure point
Revised MR16DDT shows improved reliability but chain issues persist
Facelift models command higher used values, affecting replacement decisions
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014-2015 Nissan Juke engine replacement
years.ticker: Juke 1.2 DIG-T timing chain • 2014 facelift engine compatibility • H5FT turbo oil feed

years.preview: F15 Matures: Nismo RS Performance Peak
years.description: Nismo RS launched with 215hp MR16DDT, representing the pinnacle of first-gen Juke performance and specialist replacement demand.
keyChanges: Nismo RS introduced with Autech-tuned MR16DDT producing 215hp
Limited-slip differential and chassis upgrades for RS variant
Final MR16DDT production revisions for improved durability
mainEngines: MR16DDT - 1.6L petrol turbo (215hp Nismo RS, 190hp standard)
knownFor: Nismo RS engines command premium prices due to performance spec
MR16DDT rod bearing failures peak in this production period
H5FT timing chain issues well-documented in owner forums
years.cta: Enquire about Nismo RS Nissan Juke engine availability
years.ticker: Nismo RS 215hp engine • MR16DDT spun bearing • Juke 1.2 turbo replacement cost

years.description: Final first-generation Juke models with matured engine specifications; last chance for MR16DDT and K9K replacements before second-gen launch.
keyChanges: Final production year for first-generation F15 platform
No major engine changes; focus on trim and equipment updates
Increased demand for used/rebuilt engines as models age
knownFor: High-mileage examples driving replacement demand
MR16DDT timing chain kits now standard preventative maintenance
K9K diesel values hold well due to economy focus
years.cta: Compare late-model Nissan Juke engine prices

years.preview: Present - F16 Second Generation: HR10DDT 1.0 DIG-T Era
years.description: All-new second-generation Juke adopts downsized 1.0L HR10DDT turbo across the range, with mild-hybrid technology on higher trims.
keyChanges: New CMF-B platform replaces F15; all engines now 1.0L turbo
HR10DDT three-cylinder turbo with 12V MHEV option on Tekna+
No diesel or larger petrol options; simplified powertrain strategy
mainEngines: HR10DDT MHEV - 1.0L petrol turbo with 12V mild-hybrid (117hp)
knownFor: Early HR10DDT units show timing chain tensioner wear concerns
MHEV system adds complexity but improves refinement
Replacement market still developing; focus on preventative maintenance
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020+ Nissan Juke HR10DDT engine replacement
years.ticker: 2020 Juke 1.0 engine • HR10DDT timing chain • Juke MHEV battery replacement

faq.h2: Nissan Juke Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Nissan Juke engine in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Nissan Juke engines typically cost £850-£3,200 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. MR16DDT turbo units sit at the higher end due to complexity, while K9K diesel and HR16DE petrol are more affordable.
keyPoints: MR16DDT 1.6 turbo rebuilt: £1,200-£3,200 supply-only
K9K 1.5 dCi diesel rebuilt: £850-£2,100 supply-only
Supply & fit labour adds £400-£800 depending on location
All rebuilt engines include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Nissan Juke engine prices for your variant

items.question: What are the most common problems with the Nissan Juke 1.2 DIG-T engine?
items.answer: The H5FT 1.2 DIG-T is prone to timing chain stretch causing cold-start rattling, and turbo oil feed pipe blockages leading to turbo failure if not addressed proactively.
keyPoints: Timing chain stretch typically appears 40k-75k miles
Blocked turbo oil feed causes smoke and power loss
Carbon buildup on intake valves affects performance
Preventative chain kit replacement costs £850-£1,400 specialist
items.cta: Get quotes for H5FT Nissan Juke engine replacement

items.question: Is the Nissan MR16DDT 1.6 turbo engine reliable?
items.answer: The MR16DDT offers strong performance but has documented timing chain and rod bearing vulnerabilities; proactive maintenance or upgraded rebuilt units significantly improve long-term reliability.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner wear causes rattle 50k-90k miles
Rod bearing failure can occur if oil starvation happens
Upgraded rebuilt units with improved bearings resolve known issues
Regular oil changes with correct spec critical for longevity
items.cta: Compare upgraded rebuilt MR16DDT Nissan Juke engines

items.question: Will a Nissan Qashqai MR16DDT engine fit my Juke?
items.answer: Yes, the MR16DDT engine code is shared between Juke and Qashqai (2010-2019), but ECU mapping, mounts and ancillaries may differ - always verify compatibility via registration before ordering.
keyPoints: MR16DDT block architecture identical across Nissan models
ECU software, wiring loom and mounts may require adaptation
Turbo, exhaust and intake routing can differ by model
Use our registration check to confirm exact fitment compatibility
items.cta: Check MR16DDT compatibility for your Nissan Juke

items.question: What causes MR16DDT rod bearing failure in the Nissan Juke?
items.answer: Rod bearing failure typically stems from oil starvation due to clogged turbo oil feed lines or timing chain failure causing metal debris in the oil, accelerating bearing wear.
keyPoints: Blocked turbo oil feed starves bearings of lubrication
Timing chain failure releases metal particles into oil system
Low oil levels or incorrect oil spec accelerate wear
Upgraded rebuilt engines include improved oil pumps and bearings
items.cta: Get quotes for rod-bearing-upgraded MR16DDT engines

items.question: When should I replace the timing chain on my Nissan Juke?
items.answer: Replace the timing chain at first sign of cold-start rattling or as preventative maintenance around 60k-80k miles on MR16DDT and H5FT engines to avoid catastrophic valve timing failure.
keyPoints: Listen for metallic rattling on cold start as early warning
Preventative replacement recommended 60k-80k miles
Use upgraded chain kits with revised tensioner design
Specialist replacement costs £750-£1,400 vs £3k+ for engine failure
items.cta: Compare timing chain kit options for Nissan Juke engines

items.question: How do I find the engine code for my Nissan Juke?
items.answer: Your engine code is stamped on the engine block (visible near the gearbox) and listed on your V5C logbook under "Engine Number" - or enter your registration above for instant identification.
keyPoints: V5C logbook lists engine number/code under section D.2
Engine code stamped on block near gearbox housing
Common codes: MR16DDT, H5FT, K9K, HR16DE, HR10DDT
Our registration lookup instantly identifies exact engine code
items.cta: Identify your Nissan Juke engine code instantly

items.question: Can I upgrade my Nissan Juke 1.2 DIG-T to a 1.6 DIG-T engine?
items.answer: While physically possible, swapping H5FT 1.2 for MR16DDT 1.6 requires ECU, wiring, exhaust and mounting modifications - often more cost-effective to replace with a rebuilt same-code engine.
keyPoints: Engine mounts, ECU and wiring differ between H5FT and MR16DDT
Exhaust, intake and turbo routing require custom fabrication
MOT and insurance implications for engine swaps
Rebuilt same-code engine offers simpler, warranty-backed solution
items.cta: Compare rebuilt H5FT vs MR16DDT Nissan Juke engine options

items.question: What warranty comes with reconditioned Nissan Juke engines?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty; some specialists offer extended terms up to 24 months for remanufactured units.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Keep service records and use correct oil spec to maintain coverage
Confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: View warranty terms for Nissan Juke engine suppliers

items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with 100+ vetted UK specialists offering supply-only or supply & fit services nationwide - enter your registration for tailored local quotes within hours.
keyPoints: Network covers all UK regions with local fitting options
Specialists vetted for quality, warranty terms and customer reviews
Compare multiple quotes side-by-side before committing
Nationwide delivery available for supply-only orders
items.cta: Find Nissan Juke engine suppliers near you
